behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/composition layer -> summary input builder. A Unit test of only the merge function can miss wiring bugs (wrong adapter key, dropped field, overwrite order). Real OS/network is not required because in-process fakes for the three source adapters can reproduce this failure. No dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real collection/orchestration modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel payloads; execute one report build and assert the exact summary-generation input contains all three source sections/fields with correct mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestrator -> report composer). Unit is too low because the bug appears at handoff/state propagation. System was considered and rejected: behavior text does not require real network/OS failure mechanics, only correct fallback behavior when a source is unavailable, which can be reproduced by an in-process failing adapter stub.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two adapters returning data and one adapter raising an unavailability exception; assert process completes, output contains `data unavailable` note in only that section, and other sections are present.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level rejected because real HTTP/network semantics are not required by the behavior wording.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s generated content fidelity (what it chooses to mention, omit, or distort). Deterministic stubs only test wrapper code and will hide real failures in model judgment. Not Workflow because this behavior evaluates a single generation step’s output quality, not multiple agentic decisions chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed dataset with known key values and run the real summary model repeatedly under production prompt settings; score outputs against factual checks (key metrics/issues/deploy counts present and numerically correct) and fail if accuracy drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans real external boundaries: data-source API calls plus actual email delivery to inbox. Mocked transports can hide auth/TLS/SMTP/provider formatting and delivery-path failures. Agentic was considered and rejected because this behavior’s pass criterion is end-to-end delivery/inbox receipt, not evaluation of model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full scheduled report flow against reachable test instances/endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify one email is delivered to configured recipient, subject/body format is correct, and report content is present end-to-end.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher Agentic level rejected: behavior does not require judging model quality, only successful end-to-end delivery.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is orchestration/state handoff across modules: delivery failure handling -> local persistence -> retry state consumed on next run. This can be reproduced with real local filesystem plus an in-process email sender stub that fails then succeeds. System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require validating real SMTP/network semantics; it requires correct fallback and retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run cycle 1 with email sender stub raising unreachable error; assert report file persisted locally and pending-delivery state recorded. Run cycle 2 with sender succeeding; assert persisted report is retried/sent and local pending artifact is cleared.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration retained after brake check: no mandatory real network/subprocess, no model-quality dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
